suspension_reason: The exact definition-to-first-call product measurement is 95 frames or 1900 ms, and the single authorized identity-restore attempt failed the fixed slice and runtime-bank capacity gates. The previous 260-ms acceptance covered only definitions-free warm sequences; promotion now requires the owner's explicit dated-exception-or-C1-rollback decision.

performance_decision.measurement_rule: future latency gates separate in-product time from transport and harness time

rejected_alternatives.independent_install_region: strict link overlaps the fixed overlay by 92 bytes; size-neutral shared-stack variant fails the nested eval seam and was fully rolled back
rejected_alternatives.resident_compiler_default: leaves 15741 bytes EXT and misses the 16384-byte floor by 643 bytes
rejected_alternatives.identity_bound_restore: the one bounded attempt grows l65m-commit-00 to 1895 bytes and the C1 lifetime to 1908 bytes against independent 1792-byte limits; l65m-commit-01 also adds 512 packed bytes to a runtime bank with only 64 bytes free

promotion_conditions.probe_11_implementation_and_capacity: required; canonical promotion repins provenance-bound build IDs and therefore creates a new product identity for the mandatory fresh evidence chain

claim_limit: This document reissues the measured C1 capacity deltas under the bound dated latency exception. The 1.90-second definition-call path remains a failed performance target whose stability—not acceptability—is gated. This authorization does not itself promote a product set, preserve the stopped G5 run, or claim R4/R5/R6/G6 acceptance.
